nursing_diagnosis: Excess Fluid Volume r/t compromised cardiac function
goal: Patient will have weight decrease of 0.5kg per day
interventions: Monitor I&O strict; daily weights; administer diuretics as ordered; low sodium diet; elevate legs; monitor lung sounds q4h

nursing_diagnosis: Decreased Cardiac Output r/t altered heart rhythm
goal: Patient will maintain BP within normal limits and HR < 100
interventions: Cardiac monitoring; record rhythm strip q shift; administer cardiac meds as ordered; monitor for signs of decreased perfusion

nursing_diagnosis: Risk for Deficient Fluid Volume r/t GI bleeding
goal: Patient will maintain Hgb > 8 and no active bleeding
interventions: Monitor stools for melena; check Hgb q12h; maintain IV access; blood type and crossmatch on file; assess for signs of hypovolemia

nursing_diagnosis: Excess Fluid Volume r/t heart failure exacerbation
goal: Patient will lose 1kg weight and have improved breath sounds
interventions: Strict I&O; daily weights; low sodium diet; diuretics per order; oxygen therapy; elevate HOB 30 degrees; skin assessment q shift for sacral pressure injury

nursing_diagnosis: Impaired Gas Exchange r/t CHF and COPD
goal: Patient will maintain SpO2 > 92% and RR 12-20
interventions: Oxygen therapy per order; incentive spirometry q2h while awake; nebulizer treatments as ordered; position HOB elevated 45 degrees; monitor ABGs; assess respiratory status q4h

nursing_diagnosis: Decreased Cardiac Output r/t heart failure
goal: Patient will achieve dry weight and clear lung sounds
interventions: Continue diuretic therapy; daily weights; I&O monitoring; cardiac diet; activity as tolerated; discharge planning education

goal: Patient will have clear breath sounds and be afebrile x 24h
interventions: Antibiotics per order; incentive spirometry q2h; chest physiotherapy; monitor temperature q4h; encourage fluids; culture results pending

nursing_diagnosis: Acute Pain r/t low back injury
interventions: Pain medication per order; position of comfort; apply heat as ordered; referral to PT; assess pain with VS; encourage gentle mobility

nursing_diagnosis: Risk for Decreased Cardiac Tissue Perfusion r/t HTN
goal: Patient will achieve BP < 140/90 on current medication regimen
interventions: Administer antihypertensives per order; monitor BP q4h; low sodium diet; patient education on HTN management; assess for headache or vision changes

nursing_diagnosis: Impaired Physical Mobility r/t hip fracture
goal: Patient will participate in PT and ambulate with walker by day 3
interventions: Pain management before PT; assist with repositioning q2h; anti-embolism stockings; DVT prophylaxis per order; PT/OT consultation; fall precautions

nursing_diagnosis: Impaired Gas Exchange r/t pulmonary embolism
goal: Patient will maintain SpO2 > 93% and report decreased dyspnea
interventions: Heparin drip per protocol; monitor aPTT q6h; supplemental O2 per order; continuous pulse ox; strict bedrest; assess for bleeding; emotional support for anxiety

nursing_diagnosis: Decreased Cardiac Output r/t right heart strain from PE
goal: Patient will demonstrate hemodynamic stability with MAP > 65
interventions: Heparin drip per protocol; continuous hemodynamic monitoring; monitor troponin trends; supplemental O2; strict bedrest; assess for signs of RV failure; pain management

nursing_diagnosis: Risk for Ineffective Cerebral Tissue Perfusion r/t head injury
goal: Patient will maintain GCS 15 and have no neurological deterioration
interventions: Neuro checks q2h; CT results reviewed - negative; monitor for vomiting or worsening headache; concussion precautions education; discharge with head injury instructions

goal: Patient will be afebrile and report decreased dysuria within 24h
interventions: Antibiotics per order; encourage PO fluids 2L/day; monitor temperature q4h; urine culture pending; pain management for dysuria
